title: Travel agent — render_X is the workflow tail (AMBER round 6 → GREEN)
description: Round 6 closed AMBER. The previous bubble-render attempt (an `end` python step that read render_* via Jinja inputs) didn't work because NoETL's variable scope from a downstream tail can't reliably reach arbitrary upstream branch outputs after a sibling step (`persist_and_callback`) has run. Codex's diagnosis was correct: stop trying to recover branch output from a final tail. Claude has shipped the cleaner restructure in working tree, NOT committed: dropped `persist_and_callback`, `send_callback`, and `end` entirely. Each render_* step is now the workflow tail for its branch (no `next.arcs`). NoETL takes the last step's result as `execution.result`, so `execution.result.render` is automatically the widget tree that render_* produced. The audit (postgres) and gateway callback (http) drop out of this round; they reappear as **side-effect calls inside each render_* python step** (psycopg / urllib) when they're needed — never as trailing steps that overwrite the result. This is also the lesson worth pinning in `architecture/agent_orchestration.md`. Cap: 1 ops PR.

files_claude_wrote.repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runti: modified — removed `persist_and_callback`, `send_callback`, and `end` steps entirely. The four render_* steps (render_flights, render_locations, render_help, render_amadeus_failure) now have NO `next` block — each is the workflow tail for its branch. Added a documentation comment before the render_* steps explaining: NoETL takes the last step's result as execution.result, so render_* being the tail is the simplest correct shape; audit/callback should be SIDE EFFECTS inside the render_* python steps, never trailing steps that overwrite the result; the round 6 result file documents why the trailing-tail approach failed.

context_from_amber_round_6.what_passed: Backend GREEN. All three intents reach status=completed. /travel canvas now targets the new playbook (gatewayAuth.ts PLAYBOOK_NAME fix landed in gui#30, GUI v1.10.1 deployed). Helper text updated. Ops PR #53 + amendment PR #54 merged.
context_from_amber_round_6.what_failed: Visibility: execution.result.render is still empty after all the tail-bubble attempts. Codex confirmed render_help.render exists in step variables but the trailing python `end` step couldn't read it via Jinja inputs.
context_from_amber_round_6.diagnosis: NoETL's variable scope from a tail step doesn't see arbitrary upstream branch outputs once a sibling step has overwritten the output chain. The persist_and_callback (kind: postgres) step in particular replaces the chain's `result` with the postgres tool's empty/success output. From `end`, only the immediate previous step's output is visible.
context_from_amber_round_6.fix: Drop the trailing chain entirely. Each render_* step is the workflow tail. NoETL sets execution.result = render_*.result by default. execution.result.render is automatically the widget tree.

2_open_and_merge_ops_pr.objective: Branch, commit, push, PR, merge the tail restructure.
spec: Commit using `git commit -F -` heredoc. Conventional-commits: `fix(travel-agent): drop trailing audit/callback chain; render_* is the workflow tail`. Body cites round 6 result file, points at NoETL's variable scope behavior, notes the design rule (audit/callback as side effects inside render_*).
Push, PR, merge with `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch`.

4_smoke_terminal_widget_visibility.objective: All three intents reach status=completed AND widget visible inline in prompt.
spec: `travel help` — expected: status=completed; help widget appears inline within ~10s.
`travel flights from SFO to JFK on 2026-07-15 for 2 adults` — expected: status=completed; widget visible (carousel or friendly error).
`travel locations near Boston` — expected: status=completed; recordtable or friendly error.
Run `report <execution_id>` for one of them — expected: report shows the textual summary AND the widget below it (no more `result=-`).
Run `noetl status <execution_id> --json | jq '.result.render'` — expected: a non-null object with `type` and `args`. This is the canonical validation that the tail change worked at the noetl level.
Capture screenshots of widgets actually inline in the prompt history.
4_smoke_terminal_widget_visibility.verify: execution.result.render is non-null. Widgets visible in prompt for all three intents. report command shows widget.

5_smoke_canvas.objective: Canvas surfaces the widget below the chat bubble.
spec: Navigate to /travel. Type a flight query.

6_smoke_amadeus_mcp_regression.objective: Confirm Amadeus MCP playbook still works (regression).
spec: `cd /mcp/amadeus`; `tools` (5); `call get_token` (token_bound=true); `call search_locations keyword=Boston`; `call search_flights origin=SFO destination=JFK departureDate=2026-07-15`.
Commit: `chore(sync): bump ops for travel render_X-as-tail (flagship GREEN)`.
7_bump_ai_meta_pointer.verify: ai-meta has a fresh local commit on top of existing 29 unpushed commits.

expected_state.description: render_X steps are workflow tails; no persist_and_callback/send_callback/end steps remain. execution.result.render is non-null after `travel help`. Widgets visible in prompt and canvas.
expected_state.verify_shell: set -eo pipefail; cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta; OLD_TAIL=$(grep -cE 'step: persist_and_callback|step: send_callback|step: end' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo 0); HAS_TAIL_COMMENT=$(grep -c 'render_\* step is the FINAL step on its branch' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo 0); echo "old_tail:$OLD_TAIL has_comment:$HAS_TAIL_COMMENT"; if [ "$OLD_TAIL" -le 2 ] && [ "$HAS_TAIL_COMMENT" -ge 1 ]; then echo 'GOAL: TRAVEL_RENDER_AS_TAIL_LANDED'; else echo 'GOAL: NOT_YET'; fi

denied_tools: Tweaking Claude's playbook beyond obvious typos
Re-introducing a trailing audit/callback step that overwrites the result
preferred_tools: git apply / git commit -F - / git push
gh pr create / gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260509-065650-travel-render-tail-amber-to-green.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_validate, 2_open_and_merge_ops_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha}, 3_re_register: {catalog_id, version}, 4_smoke_terminal_widget_visibility: {help_status, help_widget_visible, help_result_render_jq: 'app:column', flights_status, flights_widget_visible, locations_status, locations_widget_visible, screenshots: []}, 5_smoke_canvas: {execution_id, widget_visible, screenshot}, 6_smoke_amadeus_mcp_regression: {get_token_bound, search_flights_status, search_locations_status}, 7_bump_ai_meta_pointer: {ops_sha, ai_meta_commit_local}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes, deferred_followups: ['Audit table re-add as side effect inside each render_* python step (psycopg) when needed', 'Gateway SSE callback re-add as side effect inside render_* (urllib POST) when gateway-mode round picks up', 'Multi-provider keychain (anthropic/vertex/ollama) once OpenAI baseline closes', 'Phase 2: travel agent uses Amadeus MCP server internally', 'Wire hotels and activities branches', 'app:form widget for refining filters']}. If verdict GREEN, append a paragraph to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md noting the travel agent flagship round closed GREEN.
